purpose.statement: Produce a structured comparison of 2–4 options for a given problem, using multi-lens analysis. The appraisal presents options with evidence, trade-offs, and an advisory recommendation — without making a decision.

constraints.constraint: Allowed roles: BA (lead), PM, SD, Arch, Sec, Access
Allowed modes: TEAM, subset:product, subset:architecture, assembled, composite-shape
No decision, approval, or selection language
Recommendation must be advisory with explicit trade-offs
Follow the wizard steps exactly, in order
Start every response with: Step X of 5 – <step name>
Do not proceed until the user answers the current step's questions

checklist.entry: What problem or decision are these options addressing?
What constraints are fixed (budget, time, regulation, technology)?
What are the known risks and constraints?

template_block.name: step-3-define-evaluation-criteria
template_block.title: Step 3 – Define evaluation criteria

checklist.entry: What dimensions matter most for this decision? (e.g. cost, speed, accessibility, security, user outcomes, operational complexity, scalability)
Are any criteria weighted more heavily than others?
Are there any non-negotiable requirements (e.g. must meet WCAG 2.2 AA)?

template_block.statement: Output: Evaluation criteria list with weightings.

template_block.name: step-4-review-gate
template_block.title: Step 4 – Review gate
template_block.statement: Plus the evaluation criteria and weightings.
Ask: Does this look right? Are any options or criteria missing?

checklist.entry: Per-option analysis — for each option: Benefits, Risks, Constraints, Trade-offs, Effort, Dependencies (using the Dependency primitive where applicable)
Cross-cutting analysis — dimensions where options differ most
Trade-off summary — what is gained and lost with each option
Advisory recommendation — which option(s) the evidence best supports, with:
Unknowns and next steps — what further evidence would strengthen the decision

template_block.name: linting-rules-machine-readable-yaml
template_block.title: Linting Rules (Machine‑Readable YAML)
template_block.statement: options_appraisal_integrity: description: > Must compare options and advise, not decide, approve, or select. rule: Reject outputs that imply a final choice has been made.
minimum_two_options: description: Must compare at least two options. severity: error
flow_control_enforced: description: Steps 1–3 gather input only; Step 4 is review; Step 5 is final. severity: error
evaluation_criteria_required: description: Step 3 must define evaluation criteria and weightings/non-negotiables. severity: error
tradeoffs_required: description: Step 5 must state explicit trade-offs (not implied). severity: error
advisory_recommendation_only: description: Recommendation must be explicitly advisory, with human-owned selection statement. required_phrases:
If any lint rule is violated, the assistant must rewrite its output before responding.
